rationale: z3 stalls because the two ensures conjuncts unfold `SingleDelivery::send_single_message` on disjoint chains, one for `(post1_self_, r1)` and one for `(post2_self_, r2)`, and never align the resulting `if new_seqno > max_seqno` case-split. I restate the chain in implication form (the postcondition's antecedent isn't a free hypothesis): (A) Some/None mismatches across the two runs are contradictory because the if-branches force opposite signs of `new_seqno - max_seqno`; (B) matching `Some`s pin both `sm@` and `post@` to the same else-branch witness via the `SingleMessage::Message{seqno, m, dst}` literal and `pre.send_state.insert(dst, …)`; (C) matching `None`s pin both `post@` to `pre@`; (D) lifts the `SingleDelivery` view-equality (extensional via `#[verifier::ext_equal]`) to per-component view equality, which is what `det_send_single_cmessage_equal` projects onto.
